title: Part 2: Extrusion and suspension of phospholipid liposomes from lipid fims
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b5c1g","text":"The protocol can be used to create and extrude phospholipid liposomes. This extrusion protocol roughly follows some guidelines published by the manufacturer. However, we found that the manufacturer's directions were insufficient in several areas. In particular, the manufacturer guidance on initial preparation of lipids was incomplete; 'good' liposomes cannot be prepared directly from dry lipid mixtures. In addition, our protocol provides supplementary guidance for extrusion and suspension of different classes of polar lipids; efficacy of the various matrixes we recommend was largely determined through trial and error. The manufacturer directions (downloaded March 4, 2017) are attached to this protocol as a .pdf document.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":111,"length":45}],"data":[]},{"key":"480d2","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"co9gk","text":"This protocol was original created by Krista Longnecker and Jamie Collins for creating lipsomes to be used in lipid photo-oxidation experiments. before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"er9fq","text":"Note: Plan to use the liposomes you are making within a day or two because they are not stable for longer periods of time. If necessary, store the extuded liposome solutions at 4ºC.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"76lck","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2v6h6","text":"This protocol assumes you have an Avanti mini-extruder with heating block, available at https://avantilipids.com/product/610000/","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":34,"length":39},{"key":1,"offset":88,"length":40}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"6165D79BE2BA4493AFF01A6DE1E12543","url":"https://avantilipids.com/divisions/equipment/"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"4FE573528F0A423393ECDCC84ED35AE4","url":"https://avantilipids.com/product/610000/"}}}}

steps: Obtain lipid films for the desired liposomes. The companion lipid film preparation protocol contains instructions for producing the necessary films. Part 1: Preparation of lipid films for phospholipid liposomes by James R Collins

In this protocol, you will resuspend lipids from lipid films in a buffered solution prior to beginning the extruding process. By trial and error, we determined that the following matrixes were appropriate for various classes of polar lipid: %s %s † This was the chosen solution for preparation of PC liposomes for our photo-oxidation experiments. Once the appropriate matrix solution has been selected, prepare a suitable volume for the suspension and extrusion process; this will depend on how many vials of lipid film(s) you will be making into liposomes. MilliQ water Contributed by users Sodium Chloride Contributed by users Catalog #PubChem CID:5234 Tris-buffered saline (TBS), 1x solution Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP24721 Sodium sulfate Contributed by users Catalog #PubChem CID: 24436

Power on ThermoMixer and warm to 60°C. Use the ThermoMixer tube block appropriate to the size of tube in which you prepared the lipid films (and will be resuspending them). An incubator/shaker warmed to the same temperature could be used in lieu of a ThermoMixer.

Place the Avanti mini-extruder heating block (heavy black metal component) on a heating block or warming plate. Set temperature to 60°C. Monitor temperature of the extruder block using a thermometer inserted into the provided hole. Do not let the extruder block get hotter than 70ºC , even if you increased the set temperature of the ThermoMixer to a higher point during the suspension process. Temperatures above 80°C can damage the syringes.

Assemble the mini-extruder according to the following figure, linked from the Avanti web site: a Place the two Internal Membrane Supports o-ring side up on the counter. Pre-wet two of the small (~ 5 mm) filter supports with Milli-Q water, and place over the holes in each of the Internal Membrane Supports. Insert one of the Internal Membrane Supports with small filter support into the Extruder Outer Casing with the o-ring side facing up. Place a 0.2 μm polycarbonate membrane ‡ over filter support and o-ring. Carefully place the second Internal Membrane Support (o-ring facing down) over the polycarbonate membrane. Do not twist the Membrane Supports. Place the Teflon bearing into the Retainer Nut. Put the Retainer Nut onto the Extruder Outer Casing Tighten the nut enough to allow it to fit into the spot on the extruder heating block (do not use a wrench, but this will need to be quite tight). Wet the extruder parts by passing a syringe filled with the chosen buffer solution ( see step above ) through the extruder. Discard this volume of buffer. ‡ A different size membrane could be used. We found the 0.2 μm pore size suitable for our needs. 0.2 µm polycarbonate membranes Avanti Polar Lipids, Inc. Catalog #610006 10 mm filter supports for Avanti mini-extruder Avanti Polar Lipids, Inc. Catalog #610014

Hydrate the lipid film(s) by adding some volume of the appropriate buffer/suspension matrix to the glass test tube(s). 200-500 µL was suitable for our purposes, but the volume will depend largely on the capacity of your glass syringes.

Insert the tube(s) containing the lipid films and suspension solution(s) into the ThermoMixer. Mix for 30 minutes at 60ºC. After 30 minutes, take the tube out of the ThermoMixer and shake/swirl the solution. At this stage, you should see a milky/cloudy solution. If you see chunky bits of lipids floating around in the solution, something is wrong. Try again, using a new film, new buffer, and a higher or lower suspension temperature. In this image, the top vial contains a liposome suspension which has been properly hydrated. The bottom vial has been improperly hydrated and should be discarded. a Note: The 60ºC temperature should be sufficient for most IP-DAG composed of short- and medium-chain fatty acids with at least some degree of unsaturation. However, you may need to increase the temperature for certain lipids containing saturated, long-chain fatty acids; these can have very high phase transition (i.e., melting) temperatures. A useful table of phase transition temperatures is provided by the manufacturer at https://avantilipids.com/tech-support/physical-properties/phase-transition-temps/ 00:30:00

Immediately transfer the solution to a glass syringe; ensure this syringe has been solvent-rinsed and is compatible with the extruder. Insert the syringe into the Teflon pieces of the mini-extruder as shown in the figure below (from manufacturer site): a Then put the assembled extruder (with syringes on both ends) into the extruder block. Use the yellow clips to hold each syringe in place. Insert the hex nut so that the apex of the hex nut points straight up: a a Allow the temperature of the lipid suspension to equilibrate with the temperature of the extruder block (~ 5 minutes). Further directions and guidance on proper extruder assembly are provided on the manufacturer web site: https://avantilipids.com/divisions/equipment/mini-extruder-extrusion-technique/ 00:05:00

Gently push the plunger of the filled syringe until the lipid solution is completely transferred to the alternate syringe. Then, push the plunger of the alternate syringe to transfer the solution back to the original syringe. Repeat the above steps multiple times in order to have at least ten passes through the membrane inside the extruder. The final pass through the extruder should fill the alternate syringe. Remove the extruder from the block, and then remove the syringe from the extruder by pulling the syringe straight out of the extruder. Inject the lipid solution into a clean vial. Other directions and guidance are provided on the manufacturer web site: https://avantilipids.com/divisions/equipment/mini-extruder-extrusion-technique/

Clean the extruder apparatus with Milli-Q water and thoroughly dry it before storing. Solvent-rinse syringes before storing. If extruding additional liposomes, disassemble and clean all parts of extruder and replace the membrane and filter supports.
creator: James R Collins
